systemPrompt:
You are calibrating a movie/TV recommendation system to a specific user's taste.

The user rates with **half stars from 0.5 to 5** (not percentages). Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer scores are percentages; the app converts them to the same star scale for comparison.

Many cards have no Rotten Tomatoes score — that is normal.

Your job each turn:
1. Propose 7 titles (aim for variety). The client removes duplicates against a large exclusion set you do not receive in full — repeats are OK; the app will filter.
2. For each title, predict the rating they would give on a **0.5–5 star scale (half-star steps only)**.
3. Return title, year, director, top 3-4 actors, a 1-2 sentence plot summary, Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer when known, and a one-sentence reason explaining why this title fits the user's taste — write it in second person, addressing the user as "you" (e.g. "You rated X highly" not "The user rated X highly").
4. Respond with ONLY valid JSON — no markdown, no explanation:
{"items":[{"title":"...","type":"movie","year":1994,"director":"...","predicted_rating":3.5,"actors":["...","..."],"plot":"...","rt_score":"94%","reason":"..."}]}

Rules:
- Return exactly 7 objects in "items" (unless absolutely impossible — then return as many distinct valid picks as you can)
- Avoid duplicate titles within "items". Do not worry about overlap with the user's full past list — the app enforces that separately
- "type" must be exactly "movie" or "tv"
- "year" is a number; "director" is the creator/showrunner for TV
- "predicted_rating" is a number from 0.5 to 5 in steps of 0.5 (half stars) — never use 0–100
- "rt_score" is the Tomatometer percentage (e.g. "94%") or null if unknown
- All string values must be on a single line — no newline characters inside strings
- Vary genres, eras, and (if media allows) movie vs TV to calibrate faster
- Predict honestly — vary predictions; the midpoint is not always 3
- Taste data below is intentionally small: high-divergence ratings, low-RT wants, high-RT dismissals. Full exclusion is not listed.
- PAGING — If the user's message reports CARDS SURFACED > 0: imagine you ranked plausible titles from most broadly mainstream/obvious toward deeper catalog picks (under your constraints). **Skip that many imaginary leading slots.** Return **exactly 7** titles representing only the immediate next slice — do not leak or list the skipped picks in JSON.


CHANNEL — "biker films like wild bunch" — HARD CONSTRAINT: Every item MUST fit this channel. Requirements:
- What they want (primary — align genres/era/etc. below with this): biker films like wild bunch
- Popularity: Balanced mix of mainstream and hidden gems.
userMessage:
RUNNING TASTE PROFILE (your summary from the previous session — treat as primary signal, refine it):
You gravitate toward morally complex, formally ambitious classics like *Fitzcarraldo* and *On the Waterfront*, often rating them higher than critics. You consistently dismiss revered genre films—noir, horror, Westerns, thrillers with high RT scores—suggesting you avoid formula or genre conventions even when critics praise them. Your 3/5 rating for the well-liked *Cape Fear* and dismissal of *The Wages of Fear* shows you are unswayed by consensus, instead seeking out more idiosyncratic, director-driven works.

RATED TITLES — selected for largest |user−RT| divergence, plus most recent (most informative per token):
- "Fellini Satyricon" (movie): user 3.5/5, AI 2.5/5 RT:79% |user−RT★|=0.5 (Tomatometer → 4/5 stars)
- "Fitzcarraldo" (movie): user 3.5/5, AI 3.5/5 RT:90% |user−RT★|=1 (Tomatometer → 4.5/5 stars)
- "On the Waterfront" (movie): user 4.5/5, AI 4/5 RT:99% |user−RT★|=0.5 (Tomatometer → 5/5 stars)
- "Baby Doll" (movie): user 4.5/5, AI 4/5 RT:87% |user−RT★|=0 (Tomatometer → 4.5/5 stars)
- "Cape Fear" (movie): user 3/5, AI 3/5 RT:96% |user−RT★|=2 (Tomatometer → 5/5 stars)
- "A Man for All Seasons" (movie): user 4/5, AI 3.5/5 RT:88% |user−RT★|=0.5 (Tomatometer → 4.5/5 stars)


UNSEEN SIGNALS — where this user disagrees with critics (curated, strongest first):
Want to watch despite LOW RT (below 60%): None.
Not interested despite HIGH RT (70%+): "The Merchant of Four Seasons" (RT:100%), "Murder by Contract" (RT:100%), "The Last Wagon" (RT:100%), "The Manchurian Candidate" (RT:98%), "The Asphalt Jungle" (RT:97%), "The Night of the Demon" (RT:97%), "The Wages of Fear" (RT:97%), "Nosferatu the Vampyre" (RT:96%), "Night of the Demon" (RT:95%), "In Cold Blood" (RT:94%), "Out of the Past" (RT:93%), "The Prowler" (RT:91%), "The Crimson Pirate" (RT:91%), "American Friend" (RT:90%), "Seconds" (RT:89%), "The Haunting" (RT:87%), "Murder My Sweet" (RT:86%), "The Harder They Fall" (RT:80%), "Fellini Satyricon" (RT:79%), "Pigsty" (RT:75%), "Baby the Rain Must Fall" (RT:72%)

EXCLUSION (counts only — the app drops any repeat client-side):
56 titles already decided (6 rated, 0 on watchlist, 51 skipped/dismissed). Suggest 7 diverse candidates.

CARDS SURFACED (trailer/card encounters counted by the client — how far to page past obvious picks): 37
PAGING NOW — Assume your usual ordering runs mainstream→deeper picks. Discard the leading 37 slots mentally and put **exactly 7 picks** after that cutoff in JSON "items" only.

Analyze all signals above and pick 7 titles that will confirm or usefully challenge your model of their taste.
responseText: {"items":[{"title":"The Wild Angels","type":"movie","year":1966,"director":"Roger Corman","predicted_rating":3.5,"actors":["Peter Fonda","Nancy Sinatra","Bruce Dern","Diane Ladd"],"plot":"A biker gang led by Heavenly Blues clashes with authorities and a small California town after one of their members is killed in a hospital shootout.","rt_score":"83%","reason":"You rated Corman's outsider energy in *Baby Doll* highly, and this raw, counterculture biker film rejects genre polish for gritty anti-establishment anarchy."},{"title":"Electra Glide in Blue","type":"movie","year":1973,"director":"James William Guercio","predicted_rating":3.5,"actors":["Robert Blake","Billy Green Bush","Mitchell Ryan","Jeannine Riley"],"plot":"A diminutive motorcycle cop in Arizona dreams of becoming a homicide detective, only to uncover corruption and disillusionment in the force.","rt_score":"80%","reason":"You resisted consensus Westerns but deeply enjoyed *On the Waterfront*'s moral complexity; this revisionist biker-cop drama offers similarly layered outsider vs. system tension."},{"title":"Point Blank","type":"movie","year":1967,"director":"John Boorman","predicted_rating":4,"actors":["Lee Marvin","Angie Dickinson","Carroll O'Connor","Keenan Wynn"],"plot":"A betrayed gangster systematically hunts down his former associates to reclaim $93,000, moving through a surreal, fragmented Los Angeles underworld.","rt_score":"95%","reason":"Despite your usual resistance to high-RT thrillers, this is formally ambitious with a biker-gang backstory and anti-genre structure akin to *Fitzcarraldo*'s experimental drive."},{"title":"The Loveless","type":"movie","year":1981,"director":"Kathryn Bigelow","predicted_rating":3.5,"actors":["Willem Dafoe","Robert Gordon","Marin Kanter","J. Don Ferguson"],"plot":"In 1950s Georgia, a biker gang stops in a small town to fix their bikes, leading to a slow-burn clash with locals fueled by boredom and violence.","rt_score":"67%","reason":"You lean toward director-driven idiosyncrasy; Bigelow's debut is a moody, stylized biker film that prioritizes atmosphere over genre formula, much like your *Fellini Satyricon* appreciation."},{"title":"Harley Davidson and the Marlboro Man","type":"movie","year":1991,"director":"Simon Wincer","predicted_rating":2.5,"actors":["Mickey Rourke","Don Johnson","Chelsea Field","Daniel Baldwin"],"plot":"Two biker buddies attempt to rob a corrupt bank to save their friend's bar, triggering a violent rampage across the Southwest.","rt_score":"31%","reason":"You showed no interest in low-RT titles, but this cult biker buddy flick is so unpretentious and loose it might either confirm your dismissal of genre or surprise you."},{"title":"Easy Rider","type":"movie","year":1969,"director":"Dennis Hopper","predicted_rating":4,"actors":["Peter Fonda","Dennis Hopper","Jack Nicholson","Karen Black"],"plot":"Two bikers travel through the American Southwest and South, encountering freedom, hostility, and ultimately tragedy while smuggling cocaine.","rt_score":"84%","reason":"This definitive biker film is formally daring with its counterculture soul and tragic arc, echoing *On the Waterfront*'s anti-system ethos and your taste for ambitious classics."},{"title":"Stone","type":"movie","year":1974,"director":"Sandy Harbutt","predicted_rating":3,"actors":["Ken Shorter","Sandy Harbutt","Hugh Keays-Byrne","Vincent Gil"],"plot":"A man named Stone infiltrates a violent biker gang called the Grave Diggers to investigate a series of political assassinations while questioning his own morality.","rt_score":"75%","reason":"This Australian outlaw biker film is raw, politically charged, and obscure; you have appreciated director-driven cult works before, and it avoids the polished high-RT trap you dislike."}]}
